preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for SaaS trial users: acknowledges they already know the product and frames the page around why upgrading to paid unlocks their full potential. Sets the Validator archetype at ease by signaling that proof is coming.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Surfaces the concrete capabilities and outcomes that paid plans deliver beyond the trial experience. For consideration-stage Validators, articulating clear value first gives them a structured framework against which to evaluate the proof that follows.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Introduces trust signals—customer logos, industry recognition, and peer endorsements—that Validators specifically seek. Placed after Value so each trust signal reinforces the capabilities just presented, building a validation loop.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Delivers hard metrics, case study data, and ROI proof points. Validators will not commit without quantifiable evidence; positioning this after Credibility layers statistical proof on top of social proof, satisfying their need for rigorous verification before any purchase decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Presents pricing and plan comparison while the accumulated proof is fresh. For trial users in the consideration stage, clear plan framing reduces cognitive load and channels validated confidence into a specific upgrade choice.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Delivers the primary CTA—upgrade/commit prompt—immediately after the Decision beat. The Validator has now seen value, credibility, and evidence; the CTA capitalizes on that validated conviction with a direct, low-friction action capture.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary paths (FAQ, contact sales, continue trial) and exit handling for Validators who need additional reassurance or aren't ready yet. Ensures no trial user leaves without a next step, preserving the conversion opportunity.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context for SaaS trial users: acknowledges they already know the product and frames the page around why upgrading to paid unlocks their full potential. Sets the Validator archetype at ease by signaling that proof is coming.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Surfaces the concrete capabilities and outcomes that paid plans deliver beyond the trial experience. For consideration-stage Validators, articulating clear value first gives them a structured framework against which to evaluate the proof that follows.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Introduces trust signals—customer logos, industry recognition, and peer endorsements—that Validators specifically seek. Placed after Value so each trust signal reinforces the capabilities just presented, building a validation loop.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Delivers hard metrics, case study data, and ROI proof points. Validators will not commit without quantifiable evidence; positioning this after Credibility layers statistical proof on top of social proof, satisfying their need for rigorous verification before any purchase decision.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — critical for consideration stage conversion goal.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility at the decision moment for trial-to-paid conversion.

slots.headline: The trial showed you what MODE does. This is what it unlocks.
slots.subhead: Full semantic resolution across all four dimensions — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode. One brief. Three variants. No manual decisions.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default and supports clear articulation of product value.

slots.headline: Every capability the trial gated.
slots.subhead: MODE Kit is the complete intelligence layer — every resolution dimension, every agent, every component, fully specified.

features.title: Semantic Resolution Engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode simultaneously — no manual variant logic required.

features.title: IA Planner Agent
features.description: Brief to full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus via your own Anthropic key — you own the spend, not a vendor meter.

features.title: Multi-Variant Generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. The right register for every audience, without three separate briefs.

features.title: Palette Map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. No manual token assignment — the system makes the right call per component per funnel stage.

features.title: Component Manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Built on Radix UI — zero new primitives to learn.

features.title: Token Dashboard
features.description: Editable palette map, accent configuration, and preset switching in one place. Configure once, adapt freely — swap presets, extend the manifest, fork the system.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype — with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Always include role and company for Validator credibility; place near a CTA as testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: We'd spent months building variant logic by hand — different copy density for different segments, manual CTA rules per funnel stage. MODE collapsed that into a single configuration. The resolution engine does what we were doing in Notion and Jira, except it's code and it's consistent.

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default for establishing credibility and momentum during consideration stage.

stats.label: Median time from install to first resolved output
Reduction in manual variant decisions reported by teams after 30 days
More page architectures shipped per sprint compared to pre-MODE baseline

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — essential for conversion goal on trial-to-paid funnel stage.
